content.claim: Early tracking may increase educational inequality; a CEU thesis using Assessment of Basic Competences panel data found increased dispersion in outcomes, but reported zero tertiary education effect, suggesting the track system may not fulfill its stated aim of better preparing academically able students for higher education.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Single study; tertiary outcomes data not widely replicated in provided literature
Does not establish whether zero tertiary effect is due to selection mechanism itself or confounding factors
Study acknowledges long-term effects could not be conclusively established
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
